pattern: Treating each allowed swap as an edge turns sortability into a union-find question: every connected block must hold exactly the values that belong to its positions.
hints: Elements that can swap, directly or in a chain, can be freely rearranged among the positions they occupy.
For a fixed k, union indices whose values AND to k; the array sorts iff every component's value set equals its index set.
Only pairwise ANDs are worth trying as k; test them from largest down and return the first that lets every component sort, falling back to 0.

edge_inputs.description: swap two values whose AND is one
swap values whose AND is two
fully reversed, only k zero works

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    nums = list(range(n))
    for i in range(n - 1, 0, -1):
        j = rng.randrange(0, i + 1)
        nums[i], nums[j] = nums[j], nums[i]
    return [nums]
stress.note: valid permutation; small n keeps the quadratic checks fast
